source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --audit-log-path argument is set

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-path argument is set
input_row.description: Enable	auditing	on	the	Kubernetes	API	Server	and	set	the	desired	audit	log	path.
input_row.rationale: Auditing	the	Kubernetes	API	Server	provides	a	security-relevant	chronological	set	of records	documenting	the	sequence	of	activities	that	have	affected	system	by	individual users,	administrators	or	other	components	of	the	system.	Even	though	currently, Kubernetes	provides	only	basic	audit	capabilities,	it	should	be	enabled.	You	can	enable	it	by setting	an	appropriate	audit	log	path. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--audit-log-path	argument	is	set	as	appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	--audit-log-path	parameter	to	a	suitable path	and	file	where	you	would	like	audit	logs	to	be	written,	for	example: --audit-log-path=/var/log/apiserver/audit.log Default	Value: By	default,	auditing	is	not	enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 96	|	P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/audit/ 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/22 Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 6.2	Ensure	Audit	Log	Settings	Support	Appropriate	Log	Entry	Formatting Validate	audit	log	settings	for	each	hardware	device	and	the	software	installed	on	it, ensuring	that	logs	include	a	date,	timestamp,	source	addresses,	destination	addresses,	and various	other	useful	elements	of	each	packet	and/or	transaction.	Systems	should	record logs	in	a	standardized	format	such	as	syslog	entries	or	those	outlined	by	the	Common	Event Expression	initiative.	If	systems	cannot	generate	logs	in	a	standardized	format,	log normalization	tools	can	be	deployed	to	convert	logs	into	such	a	format. Version	7 6.2	Activate	audit	logging Ensure	that	local	logging	has	been	enabled	on	all	systems	and	networking	devices. 6.3	Enable	Detailed	Logging Enable	system	logging	to	include	detailed	information	such	as	an	event	source,	date, user,	timestamp,	source	addresses,	destination	addresses,	and	other	useful	elements. 97	|	P a g e
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 96	|	P a g e 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/audit/ 3. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/22 Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 6.2	Ensure	Audit	Log	Settings	Support	Appropriate	Log	Entry	Formatting Validate	audit	log	settings	for	each	hardware	device	and	the	software	installed	on	it, ensuring	that	logs	include	a	date,	timestamp,	source	addresses,	destination	addresses,	and various	other	useful	elements	of	each	packet	and/or	transaction.	Systems	should	record logs	in	a	standardized	format	such	as	syslog	entries	or	those	outlined	by	the	Common	Event Expression	initiative.	If	systems	cannot	generate	logs	in	a	standardized	format,	log normalization	tools	can	be	deployed	to	convert	logs	into	such	a	format. Version	7 6.2	Activate	audit	logging Ensure	that	local	logging	has	been	enabled	on	all	systems	and	networking	devices. 6.3	Enable	Detailed	Logging Enable	system	logging	to	include	detailed	information	such	as	an	event	source,	date, user,	timestamp,	source	addresses,	destination	addresses,	and	other	useful	elements. 97	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

Given the control's context and the nature of auditing, the following points are considered:

- The audit field specifies running a `ps -ef` command on the master node to check if the `--audit-log-path` argument is set, indicating that this requires SSH access to the Kubernetes master node.
- The description, rationale, and remediation emphasize a configuration aspect of the Kubernetes API Server, specifically setting a command-line argument in the Kubernetes environment, which must be accessed and verified at the OS and application level.
- The control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters, as noted in the additional information, which indicates that there might not be an API or CLI command available to check this configuration directly from Alibaba Cloud's managed service interface.
- Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI tools do not directly expose methods to check real-time process command-line arguments or configurations that are not explicitly logged or reported by the service or management layer.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the Kubernetes Master Node:**
   - Use SSH to log into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster.
   - Ensure you have appropriate permissions and the correct private key or credentials for access.

2. **Verify API Server Argument:**
   - Run the command `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the running processes and filter for the Kubernetes API Server process.
   - Check for the presence of the `--audit-log-path` argument within the command-line options for the `kube-apiserver` process.

3. **Confirm Secure Configuration:**
   - Ensure that the `--audit-log-path` argument is pointing to a valid file path where audit logs are expected to be stored (e.g., `/var/log/apiserver/audit.log`).

4. **Document Compliance:**
   - Confirm that the audit log path is correctly set, and document your findings for compliance records.
   - Note any discrepancies or areas that require remediation, such as undefined audit log paths or incorrect file locations.
